crob-mob: Crob Mob is a meme token on the Cronos ecosystem associated with the Cronos community and DeFi apps on Cronos (e.g., VVS Finance). It is primarily community-driven and used for ecosystem participation and trading on supported exchanges.
dar-open-network: DAR Open Network is the rebranded ecosystem behind Mines of Dalarnia, focused on expanding beyond the original game into a broader Web3 network. The DAR token is used within the ecosystem for participation, incentives and governance-related utility across its products and community.
drift-protocol: Drift is a decentralized derivatives (perpetuals) and spot trading protocol on Solana. It uses a liquidity and risk engine designed for on-chain trading, and the DRIFT token is used for governance and ecosystem incentives within the protocol.
dymension: Dymension is a modular blockchain network designed for “RollApps” (application-specific rollups). It provides settlement and networking for rollups, while allowing apps to launch their own chains; the DYM token is used for network participation, fees and governance.
elizaos: ElizaOS is an open-source framework for building and running AI agents that can use tools, interact with apps and operate across Web3 environments. The ELIZA token is associated with the project’s ecosystem and is used for community/economic coordination around the agent framework.
famous-fox-federation: Famous Fox Federation is a Solana-based NFT ecosystem built around the Famous Foxes collection. The FOXY token is the project’s utility token, used for staking rewards and participation across the ecosystem’s products and community features.
gunz: GUNZ is the token for Gunzilla Games’ blockchain ecosystem that powers player-owned in-game items and economies (notably in the game Off The Grid). It is used to support in-game transactions and the broader game economy on the GUNZ network.
a-i-agent-kai-a: A.I Agent kAia is a crypto project themed around AI agents within the Kaia ecosystem. The KAIA token is used within its community and ecosystem mechanics (such as participation and incentives) as defined by the project and supported marketplaces.
kernel-2: KernelDAO is a restaking protocol that aims to provide shared economic security by allowing staked assets to secure multiple services (e.g., oracles, bridges and middleware). The KERNEL token is used for governance and ecosystem incentives across KernelDAO products.
layer3: Layer3 is a Web3 discovery and learning platform where users complete quests and explore on-chain ecosystems. The L3 token is used within the Layer3 ecosystem for rewards and participation features tied to its quests and community programs.
